factors.summary: The company is facing pricing pressures, particularly in the oil and gas industry, which is impacting its ability to raise prices successfully.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with some savings achieved through Realignment Programs, though there is a 20% expected reduction in workforce.

evidence.text: Upon completion of the Realignment Programs, we expect run-rate cost savings of approximately $230 million, of which the vast majority is anticipated to be achieved in 2018, and an approximate 20% reduction in our global workforce, relative to early 2015 workforce levels.
evidence.subsection: RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — Three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017

factors.summary: The supply chain is being improved through global expansion and process improvements, though challenges remain in certain regions.

evidence.text: We continue to expand our global supply chain capability to meet global customer demands and ensure the quality and timely delivery of our products. Additionally, we continue to devote resources to improving the supply chain processes across our business segments to find areas of synergy and cost reduction and to improve our supply chain management capability to ensure it can meet global customer demands.

evidence.text: With continued stability in oil prices, at improved levels beginning in the second half of 2017, we anticipate that customers will increase maintenance and short cycle investment throughout 2018.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategy focused on aftermarket services, global expansion, and cost efficiency through Realignment Programs.

evidence.text: The aftermarket portion of our business also helps provide business stability during various economic periods. The aftermarket business, which is primarily served by our network of 174 QRCs located around the globe, provides a variety of service offerings for our customers including spare parts, service solutions, product life cycle solutions and other value-added services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear and focused long-term strategy with well-defined goals, which should support future growth.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign currency exchange rates, oil prices, and economic conditions in the oil and gas industry.

evidence.text: Throughout this discussion of our results of operations, we discuss the impact of fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates. We have calculated currency effects on operations by translating current year results on a monthly basis at prior year exchange rates for the same periods. With continued stability in oil prices, at improved levels beginning in the second half of 2017, we anticipate that customers will increase maintenance and short cycle investment throughout 2018.
evidence.subsection: RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — Three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While the company identifies key sensitivities, there is no indication of current negative impacts or opportunities for mitigation.

factors.summary: The adoption of the New Revenue Standard is a key catalyst, impacting both revenue recognition and backlog.

evidence.text: Effective January 1, 2018, we adopted ASU No. 2014-09, 'Revenue from Contracts with Customers (Topic 606)' and all related ASUs ('New Revenue Standard'). We adopted the New Revenue Standard using the modified retrospective method for transition. For a discussion related to our adoption of the New Revenue Standard requirements refer to Notes 1 and 2 of this Quarterly Report.
evidence.subsection: RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — Three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Adoption of the New Revenue Standard is a significant catalyst that should improve financial reporting and backlog management.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its market share, supported by a strong product portfolio and global network.

evidence.text: Our reputation of our product portfolio is built on more than 50 well-respected brand names such as Worthington, IDP, Valtek, Limitorque, Durco, Edward, Anchor/Darling, SIHI, Halberg and Durametallic, which we believe to be one of the most comprehensive in the industry.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong market share supported by a comprehensive product portfolio and global network indicates a competitive advantage.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying with a focus on aftermarket services and differentiation through technology and brand strength.

evidence.text: The aftermarket portion of our business also helps provide business stability during various economic periods. factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include a strong brand, comprehensive product portfolio, and global network of Quick Response Centers.

evidence.text: Our reputation of our product portfolio is built on more than 50 well-respected brand names such as Worthington, IDP, Valtek, Limitorque, Durco, Edward, Anchor/Darling, SIHI, Halberg and Durametallic, which we believe to be one of the most comprehensive in the industry. Through our manufacturing platform and global network of Quick Response Centers ('QRCs'), we offer a broad array of aftermarket equipment services, such as installation, advanced diagnostics, repair and retrofitting.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has strong competitive advantages, including a comprehensive product portfolio and global network, which should enhance its market position.

factors.summary: Customer diversification is evident with over 10,000 customers, reducing dependence on a few large customers.

evidence.text: Our products and services are sold either directly or through designated channels to more than 10,000 companies, including some of the world’s leading engineering, procurement and construction ('EPC') firms, original equipment manufacturers, distributors and end users.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Diversification across over 10,000 customers reduces dependence on a few large customers, which is a positive development.

factors.summary: R&D investment is focused on improving operational efficiency and product development through initiatives like the Continuous Improvement Process (CIP).

evidence.text: The goal of the CIP initiative, which includes lean manufacturing, six sigma business management strategy and value engineering, is to maximize service fulfillment to customers through on-time delivery, reduced cycle time and quality at the highest internal productivity.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investment in R&D through initiatives like CIP is aimed at improving operational efficiency and product development, which should drive future growth.

evidence.text: Bookings for the three months ended March 31, 2018 decreased by $8.2 million, or 4.0%, as compared with the same period in 2017.

factors.summary: The backlog orders are decreasing across both the Industrial Product Division and the Engineered Product Division. The EPD segment experienced a significant decrease of 20.3%, while the IPD segment saw a more moderate decrease of 4.9%.

evidence.text: Backlog of $403.5 million at March 31, 2018 decreased by $20.8 million, or 4.9%, as compared with December 31, 2017.
Backlog of $819.5 million at March 31, 2018 decreased by $208.2 million, or 20.3%, as compared with December 31, 2017.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decreasing backlog orders across segments, indicating potential challenges.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with growth in the power generation, chemical, and general industries offsetting declines in the oil and gas and water industries.

evidence.text: The decrease in customer bookings was primarily driven by the oil and gas and water industries, partially offset by an increase in the power generation, chemical and general industries.
The decrease in customer bookings was primarily driven by the oil and gas and chemical industries, partially offset by increased bookings in the general and power generation industries.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed momentum across different end markets, sentiment is neutral.

factors.summary: Overall, volume trends show mixed performance across different regions and segments. While unit volumes are increasing in some regions and growing in the aftermarket segment, contributing to revenue growth, there are decreases in other regions and customer bookings.

evidence.text: Decrease d customer bookings of $25.7 million into North America were partially offset by increased customer bookings of $13.8 million into Asia Pacific and $2.4 million into Africa.
Sales for the three months ended March 31, 2018 increased by $53.7 million, or 6.2%, as compared with the same period in 2017. The increase was primarily due to increased aftermarket sales.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends show mixed performance across regions and segments.

factors.summary: The company is successfully implementing cost reduction programs, particularly the Realignment Programs, which are delivering measurable savings and are expected to achieve significant annualized savings of $230 million. Additionally, these programs are anticipated to result in a 20% reduction in the global workforce relative to early 2015 levels.

evidence.text: The decrease in SG&A is primarily due to lower charges related our Realignment Programs compared to the same period in 2017.

Upon completion of the Realignment Programs, we expect run-rate cost savings of approximately $230 million, of which the vast majority is anticipated to be achieved in 2018, and an approximate 20% reduction in our global workforce, relative to early 2015 workforce levels.
evidence.subsection: RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — Three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully implementing cost reduction programs with significant savings and workforce reduction.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed trends in its margins. While the Industrial Product Division segment is showing an expansion in gross profit margins due to lower charges and increased savings from Realignment Programs, the overall gross profit margin has decreased slightly due to revenue from lower-margin projects.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin for the three months ended March 31, 2018 of 23.2% increase d from 19.5% for the same period in 2017 . The increase in gross profit margin was primarily attributable to lower charges and increased savings related to our Realignment Programs.

Gross profit margin for the three months ended March 31, 2018 of 29.5% decreased from 31.1% for the same period in 2017. The decrease in gross profit margin was primarily attributed to revenue recognized on lower margin projects.
evidence.subsection: Gross Profit and Gross Profit Margin

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed trends with some segments improving while others are decreasing.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving across the company due to several initiatives, including realignment programs and the CIP initiative, which incorporates lean manufacturing and six sigma strategies. These efforts are contributing to lower charges, increased savings, and higher gross profit margins.

evidence.text: The increase in gross profit margin was primarily attributable to lower charges and increased savings related to our Realignment Programs.

The goal of the CIP initiative, which includes lean manufacturing, six sigma business management strategy and value engineering, is to maximize service fulfillment to customers through on-time delivery, reduced cycle time and quality at the highest internal productivity.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Management's confidence appears mixed. While they are cautious due to decreasing bookings and backlog, as well as challenges in the oil and gas industry and pricing pressures, they are taking proactive steps by implementing cost reduction programs to improve profitability.

evidence.text: Backlog of $403.5 million at March 31, 2018 decreased by $20.8 million, or 4.9%, as compared with December 31, 2017.
During the first three months of 2018, our financial results continued to be challenged by cautious customer capital investment, primarily in the oil and gas industry and pricing pressures.

sentiment.label: negative

factors.summary: The aftermarket service revenue is growing faster than new equipment sales, driven by increased customer demand for maintenance and support. This growth is evident in both the revenue increase and the rising proportion of aftermarket sales relative to total sales.

evidence.text: Sales for the three months ended March 31, 2018 increase d $19.7 million , or 11.0% , as compared with the same period in 2017 . The increase was primarily driven by increased aftermarket sales.
Aftermarket sales increased to approximately 49% of total sales, as compared with approximately 47% of total sales for the same period in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Gross Profit and Gross Profit Margin

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in aftermarket service revenue driven by increased customer demand.

factors.summary: The company's inventory levels are increasing, which may pose a risk to cash flow and liquidity. While inventory turns have improved, the higher inventory levels still indicate potential challenges in managing stock effectively.

evidence.text: Increases in inventory used $48.6 million and $27.1 million of cash flow for the three months ended March 31, 2018 and March 31, 2017, respectively. Inventory turns were 3.7 times at March 31, 2018 compared with 2.5 times for the same period in 2017.
The text does not mention inventory levels or related risks.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are increasing, posing some risk but with improved inventory turns.

factors.summary: The company anticipates steady capital expenditures, primarily focused on expanding its global supply chain capabilities and improving operations, which reflects confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: In 2018, total capital expenditures are expected to be between $80 million and $90 million.

We continue to expand our global supply chain capability to meet global customer demands and ensure the quality and timely delivery of our products. Additionally, we continue to devote resources to improving the supply chain processes across our business segments to find areas of synergy and cost reduction and to improve our supply chain management capability to ensure it can meet global customer demands.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Anticipation of steady capital expenditures to support growth and operational improvements.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable, supported by available credit facilities. However, a significant portion of the Term Loan Facility is maturing in the near term, which may pose a risk to liquidity.

evidence.text: Our Senior Credit Facility matures in October 2020. Approximately 30% of our outstanding Term Loan Facility is due to mature in the remainder of 2018 and approximately 40% in 2019 . As of March 31, 2018 , we had available capacity of $718.4 million on our $800.0 million Revolving Credit Facility.
The text does not mention debt leverage or related metrics.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable debt leverage with maturing debt posing some liquidity risk.

factors.summary: The company is committed to returning cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases, but the specifics and timing are subject to the Board's discretion based on various factors including financial condition, business opportunities, and market conditions.

evidence.text: While we intend to continue to return cash through dividends and/or share repurchases for the foreseeable future, any future returns of cash through dividends and/or share repurchases will be reviewed individually, declared by our Board of Directors and implemented by management at its discretion, depending on our financial condition, business opportunities and market conditions at such time.
The text does not discuss dividend policies or share buybacks.

factors.summary: The company's free cash flow generation is improving, which is a positive sign for its financial health. However, there is no explicit mention of free cash flow in the introduction subsection, indicating that this improvement might not be a primary focus of the filing.

evidence.text: Considering our current debt structure and cash needs, we currently believe cash flows generated from operating activities combined with availability under our Revolving Credit Facility and our existing cash balance will be sufficient to meet our cash needs for the next 12 months.
The text does not provide specific information on free cash flow.
